evals.prompt: I have a Node.js API at localhost:3000 with /api/posts endpoints. I need to validate the full CRUD cycle (create, read, update, delete) and capture evidence. How do I run the API validation? What evidence should I capture?
evals.expected_output: Run Health Check first: curl -s http://localhost:3000/health | jq . | tee e2e-evidence/api-health.json. Then CRUD: create a post, extract ID, read it back, update it, verify update persisted, delete it, verify 404. Save each response to e2e-evidence/api-crud-posts/. Evidence is JSON bodies + status codes. No response body = FAIL.

evals.prompt: Our API uses JWT tokens. The login endpoint returns {"access_token": "..."}. How do I test that auth is working correctly — both valid tokens and invalid/missing tokens?
evals.expected_output: Get token: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"...","password":"..."}' | jq -r '.access_token'). Test valid: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:3000/api/protected (expect 200). Test invalid: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer invalid.token' http://localhost:3000/api/protected (expect 401). Test missing: curl http://localhost:3000/api/protected (expect 401).
